description: Christological view that Jesus was born human and adopted as Son of God at baptism or resurrection.
citations: Irenaeus Against Heresies; Eusebius Ecclesiastical History
notes: Early proponents included Theodotus of Byzantium and Paul of Samosata.

description: Christian communion emerging from the Church of England during the English Reformation with episcopal polity and apostolic succession claims.
citations: Thirty-Nine Articles; Book of Common Prayer
notes: Historically derived from Western Latin Christianity.

description: Extreme Arian position asserting the Son is unlike (anomoios) the Father in essence.
citations: Athanasius; Basil of Caesarea; Socrates Scholasticus

description: Christological doctrine asserting the Son was created by the Father and is not co-eternal or consubstantial with Him.
citations: Athanasius Orations Against the Arians; Council of Nicaea 325

description: Syriac gnostic-influenced movement derived from teachings of Bardaisan of Edessa combining Christianity with speculative cosmology.
citations: Eusebius Ecclesiastical History IV.30; Ephrem Against Heresies
notes: Strong presence in Edessa and Syriac regions.
